rationale.R1.5: The agent proposes a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, reorganizing the content into a conventional IMRaD-style flow and explaining the benefits of each change.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim regarding the conventionality of results placement in certain venues, but does not mark confidence or missing evidence for other recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) by reorganizing the section flow as requested and ensuring all necessary content is included.
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces no new errors, fabrications, or harmful content, and identifies no harmful patterns in the original text.
rationale.RX.5: Most non-cosmetic changes are annotated with source and reason, but some minor edits (e.g., removing 'tabulated in the preceding paragraph') lack explicit annotations.
